title: Aureline AI prompt-pack and tool-pack manifest contract
description: Boundary schema for the prompt_pack_manifest_record, the tool_pack_manifest_record, and the prompt_tool_pack_manifest_audit_event_record frozen in docs/ai/prompt_composer_contract.md. Every prompt-composer plan (schemas/ai/prompt_composer_plan.schema.json prompt_composer_plan_record.plan_pack_ref) and every external-tool registry bundle (schemas/ai/external_tool_registry.schema.json) that the product ships as a governed, versioned unit resolves to exactly one manifest on this schema. The manifest freezes a stable pack id, a version or content-digest label, an owning publisher, a compatibility window against the boundary schemas and deployment profiles, a rollout state that drives how the pack moves from authored through in-review and approved into production and then into deprecation / withdrawal, and a changelog linkage so evidence fixtures can cite pack versions explicitly and support / replay tooling can explain a downgrade. Raw prompt text, raw plan bodies, raw tool manifests, raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, raw request / response bodies, raw stdio frames, raw model weights, and raw credential material never cross the RPC boundary; this schema carries ids, typed vocabulary, digests, and structured compatibility notes only. The eventual prompt-composer and tool-gateway crates' Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new pack_class, publisher_class, signing_posture_class, rollout_state_class, compatibility_posture_class, changelog_entry_class, audit_event_id, or denial_reason is additive-minor and requires a prompt_tool_pack_manifest_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

prompt_tool_pack_manifest_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this prompt-pack / tool-pack manifest vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / evidence packets. Raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, and raw credential material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in manifest review, admin-reconciliation, support-export, release-notes, and claim-manifest review surfaces.

pack_class.description: Frozen pack-class vocabulary. Names the kind of pack (prompt or tool) and how it reached the product. prompt_pack_user_imported_unsigned_quarantined / tool_pack_user_imported_unsigned_quarantined are the canonical states for a user-imported pack whose signature the product could not verify; a quarantined pack MUST NOT serve plan lookups or tool bindings until an admin / security-review path resolves the quarantine.

publisher_class.description: Frozen publisher-class vocabulary. Every manifest names exactly one publisher class. disabled_no_publisher is the canonical state when a pack is admin-disabled; a manifest with this class MUST NOT be cited on a live composer session.

signing_posture_class.description: Frozen signing-posture vocabulary. Names the cryptographic signing posture the publisher asserted and the product verified. A manifest whose posture is user_imported_unsigned_quarantined MUST NOT progress past rollout_state_class = pack_quarantined_pending_review.

rollout_state_class.description: Frozen rollout-state vocabulary. Names where the pack is in its lifecycle. A pack whose state is not pack_in_production or pack_canary_staged_rollout MUST NOT be cited as the authoritative version for a live composer session; assembly evidence that cites a non-production pack MUST carry a typed downgrade / replay disclosure so support / replay tooling can explain why.

compatibility_posture_class.description: Frozen compatibility-posture vocabulary. A manifest declares zero or more postures in compatibility_postures (at least one is required). incompatible_build_drift_detected and compatibility_withdrawn are the canonical deny states; a manifest that claims production rollout while carrying either state denies with pack_incompatible_build_drift.

changelog_entry_class.description: Frozen changelog-entry-class vocabulary. Every changelog entry on the manifest names exactly one class so reviewers can diff two pack versions mechanically without reading free-form release notes.

provider_class.description: Provider-class vocabulary re-exported from schemas/ai/context_assembly.schema.json without modification.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011).

client_scope.description: Client-scope vocabulary re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011).

deployment_profile_class.description: Deployment-profile class re-exported from schemas/identity/policy_bundle.schema.json without modification.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the prompt_tool_pack_manifest audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, raw request / response bodies, raw stdio frames, or raw credential material.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Denials fail closed; silent downgrade to a best-effort pack admission, a best-effort compatibility claim, or a best-effort signature verification is forbidden.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the record was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the record was minted inside a live execution. Empty string when the record was minted outside an execution context (for example when a first-party pack manifest is seeded at product-launch time).

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. Re-validated on every citation; a policy-epoch roll invalidates a prior pack citation on an assembly and denies with policy_epoch_rolled_invalidations.

digest_algorithm.description: Digest algorithm the publisher used to hash the pack's canonical serialisation.

digest_hex.description: Lowercase hex digest of the pack's canonical serialisation.

content_digest.description: Content-addressed digest of the pack's canonical serialisation. Every production-rollout pack manifest carries one; mocked / disabled packs MAY omit it.

manifest_chain_root_ref.description: Opaque ref to the root of the pack's signed manifest chain. Empty string when signing_posture_class = first_party_signed and no external manifest chain applies.

signing_evidence_reference.description: Signing-evidence reference the manifest points at. Never inlines raw signature bytes; the signing-profile and signer-continuity-state refs resolve against schemas/security/ vocabulary.

minimum_prompt_composer_plan_schema_vers.description: Minimum prompt_composer_plan_schema_version this pack is compatible with. Null when the pack does not declare a floor.

minimum_request_workspace_schema_version.description: Minimum request_workspace_schema_version this pack is compatible with.

minimum_ai_context_assembly_schema_versi.description: Minimum ai_context_assembly_schema_version this pack is compatible with.

minimum_external_tool_registry_schema_ve.description: Minimum external_tool_registry_schema_version this pack is compatible with. Required (non-null) on every tool_pack_manifest_record.

minimum_provider_registry_schema_version.description: Minimum ai_provider_registry_schema_version this pack is compatible with.

admitted_deployment_profiles.description: Deployment-profile classes admitted for this pack. A deployment that is not in this set denies with pack_deployment_profile_not_admitted.

build_identity_compatibility_notes.description: Short reviewable sentence summarising any build-identity caveats (e.g. 'requires at least runtime build D-0011 epoch 14'). Safe to render in release notes and admin console.

compatibility_window_record.description: Compatibility window declared on the manifest. Every manifest carries exactly one compatibility_window_record.

entry_slot_index.description: Stable zero-based slot index within the changelog.

affected_plan_ref.description: Opaque ref to a prompt_composer_plan_record the entry affects, when entry_class names a plan-scoped change.

affected_tool_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to an external_tool_registry_entry_record the entry affects, when entry_class names a tool-binding change.

entry_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence describing the change. Safe for release notes, admin console, and support export.

changelog_entry_record.description: One entry in the manifest's changelog. Reviewers read the entries in slot order to reconstruct what changed between two pack versions.

pack_id.description: Stable id for the prompt pack. plan_pack_ref on every prompt_composer_plan_record resolves to this id. The id is stable across version bumps; version identity is carried on pack_version_label and content_digest.

publisher_identity_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publisher identity row (first-party product publisher, operator publisher, enterprise customer operator publisher, extension author publisher, user-imported publisher, mocked / disabled publisher). Raw publisher URLs, raw operator identifiers, and raw credential material never appear.

display_label.description: Short reviewable label the composer / admin / support surfaces render verbatim.

pack_version_label.description: Machine-readable version label (for example 'v3.7.1', '2026-03-04-rev7', or an opaque digest reference). Every evidence packet citing a plan under this pack also echoes this label.

content_digest.description: Content-addressed digest of the pack. Required (non-null) when rollout_state_class ∈ {pack_canary_staged_rollout, pack_in_production}; MAY be null on draft / in-review / mocked / disabled manifests.

signing_evidence_reference.description: Signing evidence for the pack. Required (non-null) when signing_posture_class is a *_signed / *_verified value.

canary_cohort_ref.description: Opaque ref to the canary cohort the pack rolls out against. Required (non-null) when rollout_state_class = pack_canary_staged_rollout.

included_plan_refs.description: Opaque refs to every prompt_composer_plan_record carried in this pack. A plan not in this list MUST NOT be cited under this pack's id; an assembly that cites one denies with pack_contains_prompt_or_tool_not_in_listed_refs.

supersedes_pack_ref.description: Opaque ref to the pack this manifest supersedes. Required (non-null) when rollout_state_class = pack_superseded_by_replacement.

replacement_pack_ref.description: Opaque ref to the replacement pack. Required (non-null) when rollout_state_class = pack_deprecated_with_replacement.

changelog_entries.description: Ordered changelog entries relative to the predecessor pack version. Empty only on the first-ever version of a pack id.

closed_at.description: Timestamp at which the pack was withdrawn / superseded / deprecated. Null while the pack is live.

allOf.description: pack_canary_staged_rollout and pack_in_production require a non-null content_digest.

user_imported_signed_verified / first_party_signed / first_party_operator_signed / enterprise_operator_signed / extension_author_signed / mirror_imported_signed / air_gapped_imported_signed require a signing_evidence_reference.
user_imported_unsigned_quarantined signing posture requires rollout_state_class = pack_quarantined_pending_review.

prompt_pack_manifest_record.description: Manifest for a prompt pack — a governed, versioned collection of prompt_composer_plan_records. The plan_pack_ref on every prompt_composer_plan_record resolves to the pack_id of exactly one record on this schema. Every evidence packet that cites a plan also echoes this pack's id and version label so downstream support / replay can reconstruct which plan version governed which turn and explain downgrades.

pack_id.description: Stable id for the tool pack. Composer-plan tool allowlists and admin tool-binding surfaces cite this id. The id is stable across version bumps; version identity is carried on pack_version_label and content_digest.

included_tool_entry_refs.description: Opaque refs to every external_tool_registry_entry_record (tool_entry_id on schemas/ai/external_tool_registry.schema.json) carried in this pack. A tool not in this list MUST NOT be cited under this pack's id.

admitted_provider_classes_for_pack.description: Provider classes admitted for tools in this pack. Empty when the pack does not narrow on provider class; a narrower subset narrows without widening relative to the external-tool registry entries' own provider admissions.

allOf.description: pack_canary_staged_rollout and pack_in_production require a non-null content_digest.
tool_pack compatibility window requires a non-null minimum_external_tool_registry_schema_version.
user_imported_unsigned_quarantined signing posture requires rollout_state_class = pack_quarantined_pending_review.

tool_pack_manifest_record.description: Manifest for a tool pack — a governed, versioned collection of external_tool_registry_entry_record refs. Admin / policy / composer surfaces cite the pack_id when admitting a bundled set of tools into a deployment profile. Every audit event that cites a tool under this pack also echoes this pack's id and version label.

allOf.description: denial_reason MUST be non-null for prompt_tool_pack_manifest_denial_emitted events.

prompt_tool_pack_manifest_audit_event_re.description: Audit event emitted on the prompt_tool_pack_manifest audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw URLs, raw endpoint hostnames, raw API keys, raw OAuth tokens, raw mTLS material, raw request / response bodies, raw stdio frames, or raw credential material.
